element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn to balance vehicles, support, aircraft, and infantry roles before first deployment

element_introduction_order.element: Basic unit types and faction differences
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Crayven Corporation (heavy armor, ballistics) vs Order of the New Dawn (hover mobility, energy weapons) distinctions become apparent through early skirmishes

element_introduction_order.element: Terrain exploitation and high ground advantage
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players discover topside weakness of armored units and accuracy bonuses from elevated positions
Side, rear, and top attacks on armored units become essential tactic as enemy defenses strengthen
Infantry mortars, anti-tank rockets, improved missiles introduced progressively; limited-use powerful abilities (tactical nukes) gated by campaign progression
Infantry effectively suppress heavy units through coordinated fire without requiring damage
Shadow positioning and foliage concealment require scouting discipline to prevent ambushes
Rear-line unit placement discipline required; artillery and supporting fire demand careful squad positioning
Core constraint: troop selection before mission is permanent; squad preservation becomes strategic imperative

typical_relief_triggers: Squad preservation between missions allows replanning
High ground control reduces incoming damage
typical_tension_triggers: Unit casualties with no reinforcement option
Friendly fire risks in crowded engagement zones
Limited-use special weapons requiring tactical timing
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Deliberate, tactical pacing driven by squad preservation pressure; tension builds through information asymmetry (enemy positions unknown) and resource finality (no reinforcements); relief comes from successful positioning and objective completion rather than rapid action sequences

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to unit roles and basic terrain advantage; faction asymmetries teach differentiated strategies
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Terrain complexity increases; enemy positioning demands scouting and multi-angle assault coordination; suppressive fire and flanking become essential
notable_spikes: First encounter with faction-specific specialist units (Templar Infantry, Jaeger Infantry)
Missions requiring coordinated multi-squad tactics across difficult terrain
Scenarios demanding careful friendly fire management in dense engagement zones
Use of limited powerful special weapons (tactical nukes) requiring optimal tactical windows

notable_level_patterns.description: Objectives distributed across elevation differences; high ground control grants accuracy/sight bonuses and forces enemy retreat or flanking routes. Terrain becomes puzzle element requiring spatial reasoning
Successful missions require forward reconnaissance to avoid ambushes and identify optimal flanking routes before committing armor; scouting discipline gates aggressive action
No single unit type solves problems; armor requires anti-air and infantry support; specialists counter specific threats. Mission design forces combined-arms composition
No mid-mission reinforcements create permanent consequence for losses; squad loadout decisions made before mission create risk/reward for specialization vs. flexibility
